reviews.review_description: This is more of an interactive movie than a game, but this it does very well. You always feel drawn into the story that is told and want to know what is happening. The only thing I did not quite get was the ending or the conclusion of what happened before. Don't get me wrong, there is a conclusion to the story, its just a little weird.

Broken Spectre – Director’s Cut is a fantastic and truly unique VR experience—best described as an interactive movie set in a haunting forest rather than a traditional game. The story unfolds beautifully as you search for your father, who disappeared 20 years ago, with player choices subtly influencing the path forward and keeping you fully engaged from start to finish.

The pacing is deliberate and immersive, gradually building tension and atmosphere. Strong visuals, environmental exploration, and clever transitions—such as moments spent on the phone with the “real” world—enhance the sense of realism and emotional weight. Played in one sitting, it lasts about the length of a feature film, reinforcing the feeling that this is cinema in VR form.

Produced by one of the creators of The Blair Witch Project, the inspiration is unmistakable. Fans of found-footage horror will feel right at home, with clear influences from John Carpenter and H.P. Lovecraft, delivering an eerie, eldritch tone that lingers long after the experience ends. You truly feel like you are inside The Blair Witch Project.

This Director’s Cut has rightly won multiple VR awards, and it stands out as a refreshing, bold, and memorable experience. Highly recommended for players looking for story-driven VR that pushes the medium beyond traditional gameplay.

I found this a really atmospheric and satisfying game. There was one place that was unclear and got me stuck (as per Dev's response below) but when I reached out to the Devs they were very helpful and I believe they're fixing it. This game is more of an uncanny and spooky vibe than actual scary horror, for the most part. There were a couple of very visceral moments though that I was not so much a fan of, haha. But in general, the story unfolded well, the mechanics were good, it was beautifully moody (and beautiful to look at too.) Felt like being in a movie in a satisfying way.

developer_response.body:
UPDATE: We've patched this issue in the darkroom!

Dev team here - we're always hanging out in the Discord if you need any troubleshooting: https://discord.gg/BXFaeZhkjr

That said, we're very glad you let us know there's an issue. The puzzle in this room is dependent on arcane knowledge of how to develop analog film. Three chemicals are hiding in this room and must fill all 3 bins in the correct order. You'll need to make the light in this room red to protect the film. Once that's set up, you can tear open the photo envelope - grab it with one hand and tear the top with the other hand to open it. This should release the photos to be developed in the trays. Hope you see this quickly and enjoy the rest of the game!

reviews.review_title: BUG when trying to develop photos
reviews.review_description: Was enjoying the game, I get to the part where you have to develop the photos in the cave and the packet the pictures are meant to sit on is there but no photos near. Experience has come to a screeching halt! Please fix

developer_response.body:
UPDATE: We've patched this issue!

Dev team here - we're always hanging out in the Discord if you need any troubleshooting: https://discord.gg/BXFaeZhkjr

That said, we're very glad you let us know there's an issue. The puzzle in this room is dependent on arcane knowledge of how to develop analog film. Three chemicals are hiding in this room and must fill all 3 bins in the correct order. You'll need to make the light in this room red to protect the film. Once that's set up, you can tear open the photo envelope - grab it with one hand and tear the top with the other hand to open it. This should release the photos to be developed in the trays. Hope you see this quickly and enjoy the rest of the game!

reviews.review_title: Avoid if you’re an experienced VR user
reviews.review_description: The janky gesture controls in this game make it almost unbearable if you’re used to playing modern VR. Ignore the game’s attempt to bully you off using the controllers. Hand tracking is neither the best nor most immersive way to play it, quite the opposite. The art style is nice enough, when I’m not wrestling with the controls the story has potential, and I can forgive the distinctly last-gen teleportation movement at this price. Snap-turning only though? Another deal breaker for me. Seriously casual gamers and people with motion sickness might enjoy this purchase but I’d advise any seasoned VR user to avoid and save yourself the frustration. And avoid the gesture-based nightmare and stick to controller controllers if you do buy it.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review. We chose to prioritize immersion above all, by designing the game with the highest comfort levels and leaning into hand-tracking. It's a narrative experience that is meant to be explored carefully, and you're right to highlight that it's not suitable for players who prioritize a game for controllers/smooth locomotion/turning. I'm sorry that the interface interfered with the experience for you, but thank you for giving our indie game a try!

reviews.review_description:
Update: Still no Smooth-Turning? Oh c'mon.... 🫤
-------------------

Very Lovecraftian. Love it! ❤️👍 Keep up the good work.

Broken Spectre was already a one in a million experience - but now with the directors cut it absolutely sings. Works great even on Quest 2!

Flippin MARVELLOUS on Quest 3!

I’ve been trying to play this game but can’t get past the training in the beggining. I pick up that back pack of the hook and it shows me instruction to make a thumbs up gesture with motion left to right. I do the hand gesture but nothing happens, I’ve tried with and with out controllers nothing happens at all. I’d like to try the game but not sure what else I can do.  Any advice before I request a refund?

Even thought I am not a fan of horror games I really enjoyed the story of this game following the history of the characters  . There aren’t too many narrative games for quest so I’m very happy to have played this. The hand gestures work very well
easily my new favorite quest game, and an especially fantastic story. highly recommended!

developer_response.body: Wow favourite!?! That's incredible praise - thanks so much.

reviews.review_description: The game is a really good story and game play 👍I had a blast and loved it all,  it kinda reminds me of the flat game  The quarry. Great job 👏 👍

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for playing - we loved The Quarry and are honoured to be compared!

reviews.review_title: korean subtitle fixed, it's good game!
reviews.review_description: Interesting cosmic horror. Recommended!

developer_response.body: So glad to hear our update worked. Thanks so much for letting us know!

reviews.review_description: I love a deep story. I wasn't sure how I was going to like the way they set up the controls for movement but it wasn't as bad as I thought. I never felt lost and the story sucked me in. It got better and better and the grand finale didn't disappoint. I definitely recommend to cosmic and body horror fans.

reviews.review_title: Great story.  Wish it was longer
reviews.review_description: I enjoyed the experience a great deal. I do wish there were more gaming elements in it.  Easily would pay double the price for that!  It seems kind of a waste to have such a cool story concept wrapped up in 2 hours.  I’ve played longer games with far less story to drive things.  But yes, recommended.   (Bumping to 5 stars cuz my 4star rating decreased rating from 4.8 to 4.7 and I don’t want that)

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your review! We always wanted to spend longer on Coldblood mountain, and we hope we'll be able to expand the climb in the future. If it's any consolation you may notice things the second time that you might have missed before knowing the conclusion. Appreciate the rating so much.

reviews.review_description:
An eery premise…a father goes missing on a mountain 20 years ago but the daughter suddenly gets a voicemail from him. He needs her help on the mountain…
Things spiral from there through a haunting narrative. The mechanics of using the hand motion controls work well and the story takes some surprising turns. Took me a couple hours to finish story whichi thought was a great length. Great music to that really makes things even more unsettling. Definitely recommend it!

Played about an hour of the game. It’s an excellent project. The story had my attention and the voice work was blockbuster level

I didn’t give 5 stars because I have not finished the game and also because some of the game mechanics were not as intuitive 

But overall it’s an excellent project which I would highly recommend

This experience represents the future of VR storytelling. I absolutely loved being immersed in a filmic narrative, where I felt like an active participant rather than just a spectator. The puzzles were engaging but never too challenging, allowing me to progress through the story in two comfortable play sessions (around an hour or less each). The horror was perfectly balanced—not overwhelming or too scary, but filled with visceral moments of wilderness that showcased the natural brutality of blood and gore from injured animals and death, complemented by supernatural elements that heightened the tension. There were just enough tense moments to evoke deep emotions while still allowing me to move at my own pace. The hand tracking was impressively done and easy to pick up—the best I've encountered in a VR story. Every detail served the narrative beautifully, with no gimmicks—just pure, effective storytelling. The narrative arc was well crafted, and the use of fantastical and supernatural elements was ideally suited to the medium. Incredible work—I can’t wait to see what this studio creates next!

This is lovely storytelling (while sometimes gameified, it is more of an interactive story). The story itself is heart-wrenching and horrific in all the right ways. 

I have been thinking about this one for months after finishing it. Especially the ending. It ends on such a delightfully disturbing note.

reviews.review_title: A very nice little movie-like VR title
reviews.review_description:
This title is a great little atmospheric piece of story-telling. It's not particularly long, probably a couple of hours tops, but in that time delivers a movie-like VR experience. I think that as long as you don't expect it to be much of a "game", as in something with challenging gameplay, then it's a wonderfully creepy and oddly touching story.

The graphics are solid, but has a very nice aesthetic to them. What really stands out is the fantastic audio design. Great voice acting throughout, and pieces of music that fits perfectly.

As I said before, the actual gameplay isn't much to talk about. You basically go around exploring the environments and gather little pieces of the story on your way. There's a few puzzles, but they're very simple. The main thing here is the story, which absolutely delivers. I very much enjoyed it!

Also worth mentioning is that I played through the whole game using hand-tracking, which worked very well once I got used to the gestured required. In fact, I think that hand-tracking is *the* way to play this game for reason I can't get into - but if choosing between controller or hand-tracking, I would highly recommend the latter. It really does add to the game.
I like everything about this game. The story, the mini-games, the screamers, and the nostalgic and spooky atmosphere. That nice new layer of VR horrors.
The game will definitely be bettet as it gains fixes but the overall narrative here is really something to behold.  A fantastic single afternoon play/experience.

I was playing away falling deeper and deeper under the spell of the wonderful and engaging story telling experience with a delightful pace, some light challenges, mixed in with curiosity, and the occasional dash of urgency to not die!  Yes this is a horror experience with sensitive content not for everyone but I was forewarned and am happy to  be enjoying it with much delight.

After probably about 2 hours into the gameplay I felt it was time to lay it down for the night not because a desire to put it down but because the room around me had grown dark enough my hand tracking was having trouble working lol.
I look forward with anticipation to continue the story and see what delightful new moments are to be unlocked!
I will say as I am considering more and more about accessibility in VR and where we are at I was delighted to see this experience does provide the option to turn on subtitles varying in size, and position in your field of view.  Also while it encourages hand tracking as the primary means of engagement and navigation there is support for using the controllers if you wish.

reviews.review_title: Wow!  What a great hidden gem!
reviews.review_description: I had no idea this game existed until the dev posted about it on the oculus reddit.  I decided to check the game out and I am so happy I did.  This game is a short, story driven game about a daughter searching for her father 25 years after he disappeared.  The game has top notch voice acting and the story will keep you playing until completing it's 2 hour or so runtime.  The best part of the game is it's meant to be played with your hands and no controllers.  The control scheme works very well and I recommend you play the game this way,  If you are looking for a story driven semi horror game please check this out..

I love point & click puzzle adventures, and so far this is no exception. Very story driven but not to the point of boredom you get more invested in her plight as the story evolves. The hand tracking is good but I do prefer using controllers. Personal choice.

For the most part it isn't particularly creepy but it does have its jump scare moments. The puzzles really get you thinking without being overly difficult.

It took me a while how to use the wire cutters and shears. Basically you need to turn left or right whilst holding them.

I beat it in around two and a half hours. For the price I feel I'm just getting my money's worth, but the puzzles are a bit easy. Great for those new to the genre.

Two extra niggles and perhaps I'm doing something wrong. Firstly I tried switching off snap turning, expecting smooth movement but then it doesn't seem to move at all with both controllers nor hand motion. What's the purpose?

Secondly near the end of the game, I had to climb an ice mountain, and I couldn't. There was no blue prompt anywhere. I quit the game and reloaded it which fixed the issue and then was able to climb.

Asides from that, very good game at a just about fair price. I hope to see similar more challenging point & click puzzle adventures in the future.
Fantastic story telling, fantastic atmosphere. One of the best linear storylines single player games out there next to amazing titles such as Firewatch and What Remains of Edith Finch.

Riveting, slow burn suspense tale well worth playing. The Lovecraftian narrative leverages VR to immerse the player in a moody remote world. Avoid spoilers and experience the fun (fear) of discovering the story for yourself. Highly recommended, don’t miss this one.

More like a interactive movie. Good story, atmosphere and voice acting. Was interested the whole way. The right amount of creepy, twisted and disturbing. 
Hand tracking worked well

This is a story driven game which uses hand gestures. There's no jump scares or genuinely scary moments, just an overall feeling of upcoming dread. I haven't played this type of game before but won it in a competition. I found it engaging and played it a little over 3 days until I completed it. The production levels are high and there are puzzles which drive the story which are not too difficult. At one point I did find myself aimlessly moving around the woods trying to find where I should be going but generally everything is linear driven. The hand controls are fun but sometimes fiddly until you get the nack of them. Along the way there are "memories" that appear in the air that replay when you touch them. This was a nice touch giving context and background to the story.
I must confess to not fully understanding the ending!
If you like story driven games I would recommend this. Like many other players have mentioned, it is a quality game with good acting and a supernatural story line.

The game is interesting so far, but be warned: this isn’t an immersive VR game despite being rendered in 3D with limited ability to move in 6DOF.  It plays pretty much exactly like an old school point & click adventure game, but instead of using a mouse and keyboard, you use hand gestures.

While you can move a little, if you stray too far from the center of the play space you’ll trigger a message instructing you to step back to the canter marked by foot prints.

I’ve run into one instance where the controls weren’t explained. Eventually I figured out that to use an item on something in the distance, you have to make a throwing motion even though that’s not how you’d actually use the object.

The graphics are nice, the voice overs are professional, and while I don’t think the game intends you to do this, so far I have been able to get away with using natural turning which gives it a little more VR immersion.

The hand tracking works well.  Unfortunately I think it would have been a much better, and far more immersive, game if it had used standard VR controls and locomotion in room scale.

This is a taste of what VR games could be like on Apple’s headset. Despite the powerful hand tracking technology, it’s a big step backwards in immersion and playability.

Four stars if you’re Ok with playing a point and click adventure on a VR headset.  Avoid it if you’re not.

The game has an excellent story! I'm a horror fan but even if you are easily scare, don't worry, you'll enjoy this game without any issue.
The use of hand tracking was clever. The snap turning maybe a little too sensitive but not a big issue.
If you want to enjoy a good story (1h-1.5h) at a fair price I would highly recommend it to you.
It was good, but to short

developer_response.body: So glad you enjoyed it! We designed Broken Spectre to be a two-hour experience and tried to price it close to a night at the movies. We've heard that players who climb Coldblood Mountain a second time notice things they didn't see/hear before, and that might even double the enjoyment! Thanks again for your review.

reviews.review_description:
Well done, flaws and all!👏🏾
I’ve got a full video review up on my YouTube channel, The Lab Video Game TV, but unfortunately Meta no longer allows urls, so, 🤷🏽‍♂️😄🤦🏽‍♂️
Graphics are great but add smooth locomotion.

reviews.review_title: Cosmic Horror in VR done right
reviews.review_description:
This is an exceptional narrative experience that also happens to be an AMAZING cosmic horror story. The game hooks you since the very beginning, and thanks to its great narrative, you literally can't stop playing until the very end. I had goosebumps lots of times during the ascent to the top of this mysterious mountain.
Also, the hand tracking works great to pull you in to the story even more. Since the interactions are simple, they don't get in the way and even immerse you more in this great story. So an easy recommendation if you're into narrative and/or horror games

reviews.review_title: A good horror short story in VR format
reviews.review_description: Feels like a good Stephen King or Lovecraft horror short story captured as a VR experience. No jump scares, but suspenseful, unsettling and lingers in the mind a bit once you've completed it. Also impressed at how the controls were designed around hand tracking. I will say that I found the "gameplay"/puzzles to be somewhat underwhelming/tedious, but you really play this for the narrative/atmosphere and it fully delivers there.

reviews.review_title: Fabulous vr experience with hand trackin
reviews.review_description: Seriously brillant game with hand tracking great story fun mechanics,  only wish I could walk forward normally and not have to teleport to a particular spot. Not crazy about that teleport movement only. Otherwise excellent game

reviews.review_title: This is the right direction for horror
reviews.review_description:
I loved it. 

The hand tracking had a bit of an issue in the beginning but the hand tracking made the game much better. The controllers noise will break immersion so I do suggest trying the game without. 

The story had me hooked right after meeting the friend in the mountains and I had to know where it went. And the details of the hands changing as the game progresses was brilliant. 

Make sure you listen to all the memories along the way and explore the lore.

The story is good on this, it’s about an 80 minute play through, I occasionally got frustrated with the movement, forgetting which way I came from, and a number of times I had to recenter as my footprints where I was meant to be standing was across the room from me.
Some more interaction or puzzles would be nice , but it has good foundations to build from.

reviews.review_title: Deeper than your average VR horror game
reviews.review_description: First of all, this game is a visual masterpiece. The setting is so beautiful and you really feel like you are there in the woods surrounding Coldblood Mountain.  The hand tracking is masterful and the sounds, voice acting are all incredible.  There are thrills and chills aplenty and some truly unique and disturbing moments that will stay with me but this story goes so much deeper and connects on a very human and emotional level. Family trauma, memories, history, love and devotion- the whole spectrum is here in Broken Spectre. This game is really special and i notice something new each time I play it. Get it!  You won’t be disappointed.  It’s a work of art 💜
